title: Characters (Portal)
wikitext:
:''See [[Seiyū]] for information on the Japanese voice actors.''

Here are the main characters, secondary characters and mascot of the Evangelion TV series, movies and Rebuild films. A '†' indicates the age of a character who is deceased before the start of the TV series.

{{laptop-frame|width=85%|max-width=100em
|stickers=
{{laptop-left-sticker|image=Maya rabbit sticker.png|top=40%|left=5px}}
|screen_content=
{{CharacterListHead}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Shinji Ikari]]
|name_jp=碇 シンジ
|image=[[Image:26_C343_shinji-grin.jpg|150px|Shinji Ikari]]
|age=14
|va_jap=[[Megumi Ogata]]
|va_eng=Spike Spencer, Casey Mongillo
|description=The central protagonist of Evangelion, and the main pilot of [[Evangelion Unit-01]]. Defeating the Angel's and saving the world are the least of Shinji's worries as he struggles to overcome his own inner demons.
}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Misato Katsuragi]]
|name_jp=葛城 ミサト
|image=[[File:OP_C014_misato.jpg|150px|Misato Katsuragi]]
|age=29
|va_jap=[[Kotono Mitsuishi]]
|va_eng=Allison Keith, Carrie Keranen
|description=Nerv's Chief of Tactical Operations and Shinji's self appointed guardian. Misato weighs her private concerns against her professional duty as she works to defeat the Angels and discover the secrets behind Second Impact and Tokyo-3.
}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Rei Ayanami]]
|name_jp=綾波レイ
|image=[[File:OP_C016_rei.jpg|150px|Rei Ayanami]]
|age=14(?)
|va_jap=[[Megumi Hayashibara]]
|va_eng=Amanda Winn Lee, Brina Palencia, Ryan Bartley
|description=The First Child and Pilot of [[Evangelion Unit-00]]. Rei's quiet speech and ethereal appearance belie her greater nature, and purpose. Her connections to Shinji, Unit-01, and Commander Ikari are deeper and darker than they seem.
}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Asuka Langley Soryu]]
|name_jp=惣流・アスカ・ラングレー
|image=[[File:OP_C057_asuka.jpg|150px|Asuka Langley Soryu]]
|age=14(?)
|va_jap=[[Yuko Miyamura]]
|va_eng=Tiffany Grant, Stephanie McKeon
|description=The Second Child and Pilot of [[Evangelion Unit-02]]. Nerv's highly trained pilot ace, she is none too pleased at being upstaged by Shinji, but is still drawn to him despite herself. Her independent and hot-tempered personality masks some old and painful wounds. Her rebuild counterpart Asuka Shikinami Langley is treated as a separate character below.
}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Asuka Shikinami Langley]]
|name_jp=式波・アスカ・ラングレー
|image=[[File:Eva2-0_trailer_C16_mycom.jpg|150px|Asuka Shikinami Langley]]
|age=14(?)
|va_jap=[[Yuko Miyamura]]
|va_eng=Tiffany Grant
|description=The Second Girl, "Captain of the European Air Force" and Pilot of [[Evangelion Unit-02]] in [[Eva 2.0]]. While adapted from Asuka Langley Soryu of the original series, her Japanese surname is different, and her characterization diverges in highly significant ways over the course of the New Theatrical Edition movies. 
}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Ritsuko Akagi]]
|name_jp=赤木 リツコ
|image=[[File:OP_C015_ritsuko.jpg|150px|Ritsuko Akagi]]
|age=30
|va_jap=[[Yuriko Yamaguchi]]
|va_eng=Sue Ulu, Colleen Clinkenbeard, Erica Lindbeck, Mary Faber
|description=The Head of Project E and Nerv's chief scientist, Dr Akagi employs the world's most advanced technology to keep the Evangelions' enormous power restrained; barely. Ritsuko is Misato's oldest and closest friend, but she is deeper in the Commander's confidences—and more besides.
}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Gendo Ikari]]
|name_jp=碇 ゲンドウ
|image=[[File:Op_C013_gendo.jpg|150px|Gendo Ikari]]
|age=48
|va_jap=[[Fumihiko Tachiki]]
|va_eng=Tristan MacAvery, John Swasey, Ray Chase
|description=Commander of Nerv and Shinji's estranged father. Gendo Ikari has been given extra-legal authority by the UN to defeat the Angels by any means necessary; and he has yet more powerful benefactors with their own, darker goals in mind. But it is his own, personal goal of reuniting with Yui which he will stop at nothing to achieve.
}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Ryoji Kaji]]
|name_jp=加持 リョウジ
|image=[[File:OP C042 kaji.jpg|150px|Ryoji Kaji]]
|age=30
|va_jap=[[Koichi Yamadera]]
|va_eng=Aaron Krohn, J. Michael Tatum, Greg Chun, Sean Burgos
|description=Tokyo-3's most dashing triple agent, and an old flame of Misato. Kaji runs a dangerous game of lies, playing Nerv, Seele and the Japanese Intelligence Ministry against each other; and all while trying to discover the real truth for himself.
}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Kozo Fuyutsuki]]
|name_jp=冬月 コウゾウ
|image=[[File:OP C037A kozo.jpg|150px|Kozo Fuyutsuki]]
|age=60
|va_jap=[[Motomu Kiyokawa]]
|va_eng=Guil Lunde, Michael Ross, Kent Williams, J.P. Karliak
|description=Vice Commander of Nerv and Gendo's right hand. Fuyutsuki was formerly a professor of metaphysical biology, and was part of the UN investigation into Second Impact. Though he once tried to reveal the truth to the world, he now works alongside Gendo as the two work towards the ideals of his former student, Yui.
}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Toji Suzuhara]]
|name_jp=鈴原 トウジ
|image=[[File:OP C058 toji.jpg|150px|Toji Suzuhara]]
|age=14
|va_jap=[[Tomokazu Seki]]
|va_eng=Brett Weaver, Joe Pisano, Michael O'Connor, Justin Cook, Johnny Yong Bosch
|description=Class 2-A's resident jock and Shinji's close friend—after a rocky start. But underneath Toji's tough exterior lies a heart of gold.
}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Kensuke Aida]]
|name_jp=相田ケンスケ
|image=[[File:OP C059 kensuke.jpg|150px|Kensuke Aida]]
|age=14
|va_jap=[[Tetsuya Iwanaga]]
|va_eng=Kurt Stoll, Greg Ayres, Benjamin Diskin, Alejandro Saab
|description=Unrivaled Otaku of Class 2-A's and Shinji's friend. Kensuke is Evangelion—obsessed, and always seems to know more than he really should.
}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Hikari Horaki]]
|name_jp=洞木 ヒカリ
|image=[[File:OP C060 hikari.jpg|150px|Hikari Horaki]]
|age=13
|va_jap=[[Junko Iwao]]
|va_eng=Carol Amerson, Leah Clark, Abby Trott, Kimberly Yates
|description=The Class Representative of 2-A and a close confident of Asuka's. Though she can be stern, Hikari is very considerate of the feelings of her fellow students.
}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Maya Ibuki]]
|name_jp=伊吹マヤ
|image=[[File:OP C040 maya.jpg|150px|Maya Ibuki]]
|age=24
|va_jap=[[Miki Nagasawa]]
|va_eng=Kendra Benham, Amy Seeley, Monica Rial, Caitlin Glass, Christine Marie Cabanos
|description=Chief aide to Ritsuko with the rank of Lieutenant. Maya performs her job expertly, but has deep reservations about the darker workings of Nerv.
}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Makoto Hyuga]]
|name_jp=日向マコト
|image=[[File:OP C038 hyuga.jpg|150px|Makoto Hyuga]]
|age=–
|va_jap=[[Hiro Yuki]]
|va_eng=Matt Greenfield, Keith Burgess, Mike McFarland, Daniel M.K. Cohen, Joe Fria
|description=Chief aide to Misato with the rank of Lieutenant. Hyuga will do whatever it takes to get the job done, especially if the right person is asking.
}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Shigeru Aoba]]
|name_jp=青葉シゲル
|image=[[File:OP C039 aoba.jpg|150px|Shigeru Aoba]]
|age=–
|va_jap=[[Takehito Koyasu]]
|va_eng=Jason C. Lee, Vic Mignogna, Phil Parsons, Billy Kametz
|description=Nerv Lieutenant and final member of the Bridge Crew Trio. When he's not playing guitar, Aoba is responsible for directing operations of the most advanced military organization in the world.
}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Kaworu Nagisa]]
|name_jp=渚カヲル
|image=[[File:24 C303 col-disc.jpg|150px|Kaworu Nagisa]]
|age=15
|va_jap=[[Akira Ishida]]
|va_eng=Kyle Sturdivant, Aaron Krohn, Greg Ayres, Jerry Jewell, Clifford Chapin, Daman Mills
|description=The Fifth Child... and the Final Angel. Kaworu is sent to Tokyo-3 by Seele to foil Commander Ikari's plans, but along the way takes more than a passing interest in Shinji. The Angel of Free Will, he chooses to make the ultimate expression of his idiom.
}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Yui Ikari]]
|name_jp=碇ユイ
|image=[[File:21_C342_yui.jpg|150px|Yui Ikari]]
|age=27†
|va_jap=[[Megumi Hayashibara]]
|va_eng=Kim Sevier, Amanda Winn-Lee, Stephanie Young, Ryan Bartley
|description=Shinji's mother and Commander Ikari's lost wife. Yui's body is lost to this world, but she lives on inside the core of [[Eva-01]] and in the hearts of those who knew her.
}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Kyoko Zeppelin Soryu]]
|name_jp=惣流・キョウコ・ツェッペリン
|image=[[File:25_C297_kyoko.jpg|150px|Kyoko Zeppelin Soryu]]
|age=31†
|va_jap=[[Maria Kawamura]]
|va_eng=Kimberly Yates
|description=Asuka Langley Soryu's mother. Although her appearances in the series are limited, she has a profound impact on [[Eva-02]] and her daughter's personality.
}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Keel Lorenz]]
|name_jp=キール・ローレンツ
|image=[[File:OP C046 keel.jpg|150px|Keel Lorenz]]
|age=–
|va_jap=[[Mugihito]]
|va_eng=Richard Peeples, Tom Booker, Bill Jenkins, D.C. Douglas
|description=Head of the Human Instrumentality Committee, and of the clandestine organization Seele behind it. Chairman Keel has entrusted Gendo Ikari with bringing about Seele's ultimate and destructive goals; But he comes to find that his faith has been misplaced.
}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Naoko Akagi]]
|name_jp=赤木 ナオコ
|image=[[File:21 C136 naoko.jpg|150px|Naoko Akagi]]
|age=–
|va_jap=[[Mika Doi]]
|va_eng=Laura Chapman
|description=Ritsuko's mother and former chief scientist of Gehirn; now deceased. Naoko was responsible for the creation of the Magi supercomputer system, and the initial stages of Project E.
}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Pen Pen]]
|name_jp=ペンペン
|image=[[File:02 C152 penpen.jpg|150px|Pen Pen]]
|age=–
|va_jap=[[Megumi Hayashibara]]
|va_eng=Amanda Winn-Lee, Mandy Clark, Monica Rial, Cherami Leigh
|description=A "hot spring" penguin and Misato's pet/roommate. While some theorise that Pen Pen is more than he seems, he spends most of his time watching TV in his fridge and drinking Misato's beer.
}}
{{CharacterListRow
|name=[[Mari Makinami Illustrious]]
|name_jp=真希波・マリ・イラストリアス
|image=[[Image:Eva2-22 C1498A crop.jpg|150px|Mari Makinami Illustrious]]
|age=–
|va_jap=[[Maaya Sakamoto]]
|va_eng=Trina Nishimura, Deneen Melody
|description=The enigmatic pilot of [[Evangelion Unit-05]] introduced in [[Evangelion 2.0]].
}}
{{CharacterListFoot}}
{{reflist}}
}}

{{Character List}}

[[Category:Portals]]
html:
See Seiyū for information on the Japanese voice actors.

Here are the main characters, secondary characters and mascot of the Evangelion TV series, movies and Rebuild films. A '†' indicates the age of a character who is deceased before the start of the TV series.

Image Name Age VA (JP) VA (EN) Description
Shinji Ikari

碇 シンジ

14 Megumi Ogata Spike Spencer, Casey Mongillo The central protagonist of Evangelion, and the main pilot of Evangelion Unit-01. Defeating the Angel's and saving the world are the least of Shinji's worries as he struggles to overcome his own inner demons.
Misato Katsuragi

葛城 ミサト

29 Kotono Mitsuishi Allison Keith, Carrie Keranen Nerv's Chief of Tactical Operations and Shinji's self appointed guardian. Misato weighs her private concerns against her professional duty as she works to defeat the Angels and discover the secrets behind Second Impact and Tokyo-3.
Rei Ayanami

綾波レイ

14(?) Megumi Hayashibara Amanda Winn Lee, Brina Palencia, Ryan Bartley The First Child and Pilot of Evangelion Unit-00. Rei's quiet speech and ethereal appearance belie her greater nature, and purpose. Her connections to Shinji, Unit-01, and Commander Ikari are deeper and darker than they seem.
Asuka Langley Soryu

惣流・アスカ・ラングレー

14(?) Yuko Miyamura Tiffany Grant, Stephanie McKeon The Second Child and Pilot of Evangelion Unit-02. Nerv's highly trained pilot ace, she is none too pleased at being upstaged by Shinji, but is still drawn to him despite herself. Her independent and hot-tempered personality masks some old and painful wounds. Her rebuild counterpart Asuka Shikinami Langley is treated as a separate character below.
Asuka Shikinami Langley

式波・アスカ・ラングレー

14(?) Yuko Miyamura Tiffany Grant The Second Girl, "Captain of the European Air Force" and Pilot of Evangelion Unit-02 in Eva 2.0. While adapted from Asuka Langley Soryu of the original series, her Japanese surname is different, and her characterization diverges in highly significant ways over the course of the New Theatrical Edition movies.
Ritsuko Akagi

赤木 リツコ

30 Yuriko Yamaguchi Sue Ulu, Colleen Clinkenbeard, Erica Lindbeck, Mary Faber The Head of Project E and Nerv's chief scientist, Dr Akagi employs the world's most advanced technology to keep the Evangelions' enormous power restrained; barely. Ritsuko is Misato's oldest and closest friend, but she is deeper in the Commander's confidences—and more besides.
Gendo Ikari

碇 ゲンドウ

48 Fumihiko Tachiki Tristan MacAvery, John Swasey, Ray Chase Commander of Nerv and Shinji's estranged father. Gendo Ikari has been given extra-legal authority by the UN to defeat the Angels by any means necessary; and he has yet more powerful benefactors with their own, darker goals in mind. But it is his own, personal goal of reuniting with Yui which he will stop at nothing to achieve.
Ryoji Kaji

加持 リョウジ

30 Koichi Yamadera Aaron Krohn, J. Michael Tatum, Greg Chun, Sean Burgos Tokyo-3's most dashing triple agent, and an old flame of Misato. Kaji runs a dangerous game of lies, playing Nerv, Seele and the Japanese Intelligence Ministry against each other; and all while trying to discover the real truth for himself.
Kozo Fuyutsuki

冬月 コウゾウ

60 Motomu Kiyokawa Guil Lunde, Michael Ross, Kent Williams, J.P. Karliak Vice Commander of Nerv and Gendo's right hand. Fuyutsuki was formerly a professor of metaphysical biology, and was part of the UN investigation into Second Impact. Though he once tried to reveal the truth to the world, he now works alongside Gendo as the two work towards the ideals of his former student, Yui.
Toji Suzuhara

鈴原 トウジ

14 Tomokazu Seki Brett Weaver, Joe Pisano, Michael O'Connor, Justin Cook, Johnny Yong Bosch Class 2-A's resident jock and Shinji's close friend—after a rocky start. But underneath Toji's tough exterior lies a heart of gold.
Kensuke Aida

相田ケンスケ

14 Tetsuya Iwanaga Kurt Stoll, Greg Ayres, Benjamin Diskin, Alejandro Saab Unrivaled Otaku of Class 2-A's and Shinji's friend. Kensuke is Evangelion—obsessed, and always seems to know more than he really should.
Hikari Horaki

洞木 ヒカリ

13 Junko Iwao Carol Amerson, Leah Clark, Abby Trott, Kimberly Yates The Class Representative of 2-A and a close confident of Asuka's. Though she can be stern, Hikari is very considerate of the feelings of her fellow students.
Maya Ibuki

伊吹マヤ

24 Miki Nagasawa Kendra Benham, Amy Seeley, Monica Rial, Caitlin Glass, Christine Marie Cabanos Chief aide to Ritsuko with the rank of Lieutenant. Maya performs her job expertly, but has deep reservations about the darker workings of Nerv.
Makoto Hyuga

日向マコト

– Hiro Yuki Matt Greenfield, Keith Burgess, Mike McFarland, Daniel M.K. Cohen, Joe Fria Chief aide to Misato with the rank of Lieutenant. Hyuga will do whatever it takes to get the job done, especially if the right person is asking.
Shigeru Aoba

青葉シゲル

– Takehito Koyasu Jason C. Lee, Vic Mignogna, Phil Parsons, Billy Kametz Nerv Lieutenant and final member of the Bridge Crew Trio. When he's not playing guitar, Aoba is responsible for directing operations of the most advanced military organization in the world.
Kaworu Nagisa

渚カヲル

15 Akira Ishida Kyle Sturdivant, Aaron Krohn, Greg Ayres, Jerry Jewell, Clifford Chapin, Daman Mills The Fifth Child... and the Final Angel. Kaworu is sent to Tokyo-3 by Seele to foil Commander Ikari's plans, but along the way takes more than a passing interest in Shinji. The Angel of Free Will, he chooses to make the ultimate expression of his idiom.
Yui Ikari

碇ユイ

27† Megumi Hayashibara Kim Sevier, Amanda Winn-Lee, Stephanie Young, Ryan Bartley Shinji's mother and Commander Ikari's lost wife. Yui's body is lost to this world, but she lives on inside the core of Eva-01 and in the hearts of those who knew her.
Kyoko Zeppelin Soryu

惣流・キョウコ・ツェッペリン

31† Maria Kawamura Kimberly Yates Asuka Langley Soryu's mother. Although her appearances in the series are limited, she has a profound impact on Eva-02 and her daughter's personality.
Keel Lorenz

キール・ローレンツ

– Mugihito Richard Peeples, Tom Booker, Bill Jenkins, D.C. Douglas Head of the Human Instrumentality Committee, and of the clandestine organization Seele behind it. Chairman Keel has entrusted Gendo Ikari with bringing about Seele's ultimate and destructive goals; But he comes to find that his faith has been misplaced.
Naoko Akagi

赤木 ナオコ

– Mika Doi Laura Chapman Ritsuko's mother and former chief scientist of Gehirn; now deceased. Naoko was responsible for the creation of the Magi supercomputer system, and the initial stages of Project E.
Pen Pen

ペンペン

– Megumi Hayashibara Amanda Winn-Lee, Mandy Clark, Monica Rial, Cherami Leigh A "hot spring" penguin and Misato's pet/roommate. While some theorise that Pen Pen is more than he seems, he spends most of his time watching TV in his fridge and drinking Misato's beer.
Mari Makinami Illustrious

真希波・マリ・イラストリアス

– Maaya Sakamoto Trina Nishimura, Deneen Melody The enigmatic pilot of Evangelion Unit-05 introduced in Evangelion 2.0.
Characters
Main characters: Shinji Ikari | Rei Ayanami | Asuka Langley Soryu
Nerv staff: Misato Katsuragi | Gendo Ikari | Ritsuko Akagi | Kozo Fuyutsuki | Ryoji Kaji | Maya Ibuki | Makoto Hyuga | Shigeru Aoba
Classmates: Toji Suzuhara | Kensuke Aida | Hikari Horaki
Other characters: Kaworu Nagisa | Yui Ikari | Naoko Akagi | Kyoko Zeppelin Soryu | Dr. Katsuragi | Mr. Langley | Keel Lorenz | Pen Pen
Rebuild of Evangelion: Asuka Shikinami Langley | Mari Makinami Illustrious | Sakura Suzuhara | Ryoji Kaji (Jr)
Miscellaneous: Minor Characters | Minor Characters (Rebuild) | Extracanonical Characters | 2015: The Last Year of Ryohji Kaji
Theory and Analysis: Name Origins (Warships) | Designs | Relationships | Profiles
summary: See Seiyū for information on the Japanese voice actors.

chunks.content:
See Seiyū for information on the Japanese voice actors.

Here are the main characters, secondary characters and mascot of the Evangelion TV series, movies and Rebuild films. A '†' indicates the age of a character who is deceased before the start of the TV series.

Category:Portals
